Bug / Task Description: The `reduce(array, init, fn)` builtin (builtinReduce, pkg/stdlib/stdlib.go) passes the accumulator and current element to the reducer callback in the wrong positions: the callback's first declared parameter receives the element and the second receives the accumulator, opposite to the documented `fn(acc, x)` convention. Commutative reducers (e.g. sum) still return the right number, but any reducer that distinguishes its parameters (subtraction, building a string/list) produces wrong results.
Commit Subject: fix(stdlib): bind reduce callback params correctly
